title: Fixing Incorrect Type Inference for a Simple Generic Class

statement.markdown: You are given a simple generic class that is supposed to calculate the area and perimeter of a rectangle. However, the type inference is incorrect and you need to fix it. The class should be able to handle rectangles with any integer width and length. The `calculateArea` method should return the area of the rectangle and the `calculatePerimeter` method should return the perimeter of the rectangle. The input will be of the form `[width, length]`.
statement.inputFormat: An array of two integers representing the width and length of the rectangle
statement.outputFormat: An object with two properties: `area` and `perimeter`, both of type number
statement.notes: You should not add any new methods or properties to the class.

examples.explanation: The area of a rectangle with width 3 and length 4 is 12 and the perimeter is 14

hints.text: Check the type annotations of the class properties

editorial.title: Editorial for Fixing Incorrect Type Inference
editorial.intuition: The type inference is incorrect because the class properties are not properly typed
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution: manually calculate the area and perimeter in each method
editorial.betterSolution: Use generics to correctly type the class properties
editorial.optimalSolution: Use generics to correctly type the class properties and use the `readonly` keyword to ensure immutability
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected class will correctly calculate the area and perimeter of the rectangle

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, width: int, length: int) -> None:
